headline: Nike Shares Get a Lift After Tim Cook and Elliott Hill Buy More Stock
summary: Apple Chief Executive Tim Cook and Nike CEO Elliott Hill just gave Nike’s stock another boost.  The sneaker company's stock price rose 2% in after-hours trading after Hill and Cook, the lead director on Nike’s board, disclosed they had bought nearly 50,000 shares in Nike between them.  Cook and Hill's purchases provided a jolt of support for Nike, whose turnaround has hit snags--especially in China.

headline: Nike Stock Jumps. Its CEO and Apple’s Tim Cook Bought $2M Worth of Shares.
summary: Cook is Nike’s lead independent director and has been on the company board since 2005.  Cook bought $3 million worth of shares in Nike at the end of December.  After the purchase, Cook holds 130,480 Nike shares, worth about $5.7 million based on Tuesday’s closing price.

headline: Why the Dogs of the Dow Strategy Says Nike Is a Buy Right Now

headline: Nike (NKE) Valuation Check After Turnaround Challenges And Analyst Downgrades
summary: Nike (NKE) is back in focus after a Shelf Registration filing for up to US$2.332b in Class B common stock, tied to an ESOP related offering that could influence future share supply and employee ownership. headline: Nike Leadership Change Puts Focus On Product Reset And Investor Confidence
summary: Nike’s Chief Innovation Officer Tom Bignall has departed less than a year after his promotion. This is the fourth leadership change in this role in three years, focusing attention on Nike’s product and innovation teams. The exit comes as investors watch for signs of progress in Nike’s product pipeline and brand refresh efforts. Nike, NYSE:NKE, is contending with another leadership change just as its share price sits at $42.91 and recent returns have been weak. The stock shows a 20.5%...
